events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"in-progress","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":50,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Stressing of post-tensioning tendons is being performed before concrete compressive strength has been verified to meet the specified minimum required for stressing.","Stressing against insufficient concrete strength risks anchorage failure, concrete crushing/cracking, and tendon pull-out."],"actions":["Immediately stop stressing operations.","Obtain concrete cylinder/break test results confirming required strength (typically per project specs/PTI, often 75% of design f'c or as specified) before any stressing.","Have third-party inspector verify concrete strength documentation and approve stressing sequence.","Inspect previously stressed tendons/anchorages for damage; perform rework/repair as needed."],"rationale":"PTI and standard post-tensioning practice require concrete to reach the specified minimum compressive strength before stressing. Stressing before strength verification is a serious sequence violation and safety hazard.","references":["PTI","project specifications for concrete strength at stressing"]}
